ChangeImpactMap.description: Summary of which pinned work products and sections a source change impacted.
ChangeImpactMap.title: ChangeImpactMap

ChangedSource.description:
One source field that changed after the record was sealed. `before`/`after` are free-typed
because event payloads carry strings, numbers, or nested values.
ChangedSource.title: ChangedSource

ChatMessage.description:
One turn of prior conversation. UNTRUSTED: history is conversational context only and is
never treated as evidence — it cannot introduce sources, claims, or override a gate.
ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage

ChatRequest.description:
Body for `/chat`. `message` is the current (untrusted) question; `history` is prior turns
(also untrusted). All factual grounding comes from the freshly-assembled ContextBundle.
ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest

ChatResponse.title: ChatResponse

EvalRow.description:
Presentational EvalCase ⋈ EvalResult row for Agent Ops.

`input_class`/`expected_signal`/`observed_signal` are content-free typed signals (intent class,
rule ids, booleans, scores) for the failed-row trace drill-in — never prompt/response text.
EvalRow.title: EvalRow

EvalSourceMix.description: Eval-data modality mix (§5 F5). Fractions sum to ~1.0 across the program.
EvalSourceMix.title: EvalSourceMix

ExecuteRequest.title: ExecuteRequest

FailureTaxonomyEntry.description: One bar of the failure taxonomy: a category and how many cases fell into it.
FailureTaxonomyEntry.title: FailureTaxonomyEntry

GovernanceEnvelope.description:
The governance metadata wrapped around the decision — what makes the record *governed*
rather than a static export. The `seal` is attached AFTER canonicalization (so it is never
part of its own preimage).
GovernanceEnvelope.title: GovernanceEnvelope

GovernedRecord.description:
The sealed governed record — a point-in-time, verifiable artifact of a decision packet.
The product noun is 'governed record'; the frontend may title the page 'Governance Certificate'
as a visual metaphor only.
GovernedRecord.title: GovernedRecord

LoopRequest.description:
Body for `/actions/loop`. `approved_indices=None` uses the loop's default approval policy
(`approve_nonblocked`); a provided list approves exactly those indices (blocked actions are
still never executed).
LoopRequest.title: LoopRequest

LoopState.description: The work-loop dossier — the audit-ready record of one decision cycle.
LoopState.title: LoopState

MintResponse.description: `POST /workproducts/mint` → the new record id + the full governed record.
MintResponse.title: MintResponse

OpsReport.description:
The Agent Ops surface aggregate. Combines WS-I's three-vertical scorecard with WS-G
telemetry, eval source mix, and failure taxonomy — shape-compatible with ops.ts.
OpsReport.title: OpsReport

PermissionOmission.description: A source excluded from the record because the user lacked access at mint time.
PermissionOmission.title: PermissionOmission

PersonaReply.description:
A persona's async reply to an assignment. Carries a typed decision and message text only —
never raw sensitive content.
PersonaReply.title: PersonaReply

ReapprovalRoute.description: Route an impacted section back to the role that must re-approve it.
ReapprovalRoute.title: ReapprovalRoute

RecordSeal.description:
The server-minted integrity seal. `value` is a keyed HMAC over the canonical content —
unforgeable without the server key; `payload_hash` is a display-only content fingerprint.

The HMAC is SYMMETRIC: this server verifies its own seal (proves the record is unaltered since
it minted it). Independent third-party verification would require asymmetric signing.
RecordSeal.title: RecordSeal

RecordSource.description: An evidence source the record was built from (permission-filtered).
RecordSource.title: RecordSource

RecordVerification.description:
Result of re-checking a sealed record against current sources. THREE INDEPENDENT AXES:
`integrity_valid` (HMAC seal matches), `freshness` (sealed sources changed?), and
`approval_ready` (the deterministic gate — unchanged by verification).
RecordVerification.title: RecordVerification

RevalidateRequest.description:
Body for `/revalidate`. `event` names a deterministic WS-A source-change event
(e.g. `legal_needs_review`, `financials_v2`).
RevalidateRequest.title: RevalidateRequest

SchemaValidation.description: Result of the deterministic output-schema check (SchemaValidator, WS-C).
SchemaValidation.title: SchemaValidation

SourceEdge.description: A typed dependency between two artifacts in a SourceGraph.
SourceEdge.title: SourceEdge

SourceGraph.description:
§7 primitive. Dependency map between work products and source artifacts.
Nodes are object ids; edges carry the relation between them.
SourceGraph.title: SourceGraph

SourceVersionSnapshot.description: A source object's version + metadata at mint time — the basis for staleness detection.
SourceVersionSnapshot.title: SourceVersionSnapshot

TelemetryEvent.description:
Privacy-preserving. Raw prompt/response/document/transcript content is FORBIDDEN
by construction (`extra='forbid'`) — attempting to attach raw content raises.
TelemetryEvent.title: TelemetryEvent

VerifyRecordRequest.description:
Body for `POST /workproducts/{record_id}/verify`. `event` names a deterministic WS-A
source-change event (e.g. `legal_needs_review`); `None` re-checks against unchanged sources.
VerifyRecordRequest.title: VerifyRecordRequest

VerticalScore.description: Agent Ops vertical score shape from `frontend/src/data/ops.ts`.
VerticalScore.title: VerticalScore

post.description: Proposed follow-ups; every action carries a previewable diff or a `blocked_reason`.

Execute only approved, non-blocked actions. The plan is recomposed server-side, so a
blocked action is never executed even if its index is submitted as approved.

schema.title: Response Post Actions Execute Actions Execute Post

post.description:
Run the controlled work loop and return the dossier (assignments · replies · escalations ·
scheduled · approved_indices · audit · closed, plus bundle/brief/plan).

NOTE: `closed=True` means the loop CYCLE completed — not that every item is resolved. Derive
'Open — escalation in flight' from `escalations` + any remaining blocked actions, not `closed`.

get.description: The composed ActionPlan (typed diffs; blocked actions carry a `blocked_reason`).

schema.title: Response Api Actions Api Actions Get
Response Api Brief Api Brief Get

get.description:
Convenience GET: same dossier as `POST /actions/loop` with the loop's default approval
(`approve_nonblocked`); `closed` still means cycle-completed, not fully-resolved.

schema.title: Response Api Meeting Api Meeting Get

get.description: The core WS-G three-vertical RecipeScorecard (finance + legal + health).

schema.title: Response Api Ops Scorecard Api Ops Scorecard Get

post.description:
The DecisionBrief. `policy_gates` is the deterministic decision passed through untouched —
the LLM layer never marks a brief approval-ready (the Acme case stays approval_ready=False).

Answer a question about the meeting/decision using ONLY the permission-filtered bundle.

The LLM may draft prose, but the wrapper owns governance deterministically: restricted sources
are refused (never revealed), the deterministic gate can't be overridden, citations are
validated against the bundle, and missing evidence is surfaced honestly. Offline/deterministic
by default (no API key); history is untrusted context, never evidence.

get.description: The three-vertical scorecard + telemetry sample + source mix + failure taxonomy.

post.description:
Stale sections + reapproval routes for a source-change event (e.g. `legal_needs_review`
on `wf_approval` → approval sections stale, reapproval routed to legal).

Seal the decision packet into a governed record: decision + gate results + evidence +
permission omissions + a source-version snapshot + a server-minted HMAC integrity seal. Honest
by construction — the Acme record stays `approval_ready=False`. Runs no actions and no loop.

get.description: Fetch a sealed governed record (with its latest verification, if any). 404 if unknown.

post.description:
Re-check a record against current sources on three independent axes: integrity (HMAC),
freshness (WS-F revalidation), approval-readiness. A change event (e.g. `legal_needs_review`)
flips freshness to `stale` and routes reapproval to Legal — the record discovers the change.
